meaning: n.缝摺,活力,鼓声,船尾突出部下方,食物(尤指点心、蛋糕)vt.打摺,卷起,挤进,塞,使隐藏vi.折成摺子,缩拢
examples: The presidential contest is nip and tuck.
She'd undergone 13 operations, including a tummy tuck.
He tried to tuck his flapping shirt inside his trousers.
The time to tuck assets behind shields is when you are not being sued.
Stephanie Tuck, a spokeswoman for Esquire, said via e-mail the magazine stands by its story.
